artifacts.summary: Discovery found official NSYSU generative AI reference guidance from the Office of Academic Affairs, with separate student and teacher PDFs plus an academic-integrity PDF and official dissemination letter. No stronger central binding generative-AI policy, security/privacy policy, or approved-tools mandate was found in the searched official sources.

artifacts.claimText: NSYSU's student guidance says students may use generative AI tools to support learning activities such as clarifying concepts, editing writing, translating, practicing languages, improving critical thinking, generating inspiration, recommending papers, and improving code.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 教師不應以AI偵測工具作為處理違反學術倫理的唯一依據。由於這些工具不能完全準確地判定內容是否由AI生成，若學生之作業或報告有濫用AI生成工具之疑慮，教師應依照學術倫理則進行評估。

artifacts.title: National Sun Yat-sen University AI Policy Staging Notes
artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found NSYSU Office of Academic Affairs generative AI reference guidance for students and teachers, plus official student academic-integrity guidance and a dissemination letter. The strongest AI-specific sources are reference guidance rather than a discovered central binding policy. Rejected sources include duplicate department reposts, a library AI tools page that is only a reference list, and thesis metadata that is research output.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No login-only, CAPTCHA-protected, paywalled, or third-party source was used as policy evidence.
The generative AI sources are reference guidance; claims avoid treating them as a central binding university-wide policy unless the source language supports that characterization.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
